overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly focused academic and professional presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, totaling 3,580 followers. His content, primarily focused on computational social science, disinformation, and research tools, generates strong community interaction with an overall average engagement rate of approximately 5.32%. Strategic milestones like publications, software updates, and job openings serve as major catalysts for engagement.

contentThemes.description: Updates on detecting coordinated link-sharing behavior, platform research, methodological approaches, and specialized academic conferences.
Sharing software updates, algorithms, and technical tools developed by the research team for analyzing online coordination.
Announcing postdoctoral hiring opportunities, PhD student positions, and grant awards to expand the computational social science team.

recommendations: Maximize the larger audience on LinkedIn to cross-promote specialized tools like CooRnet, converting high professional visibility into active tool adoption.
Leverage Bluesky's strong virality (high repost rate) for time-sensitive announcements like PhD and postdoc recruitment campaigns.
Introduce interactive media such as diagrams or short video walk-throughs of tool updates to further boost click-through and engagement rates across all platforms.

platformInsights.bluesky: Achieves the highest relative engagement rate (6.14% average), showing strong community amplification via reposts on topics such as PhD opportunities and research papers.
platformInsights.mastodon: Serves as a dedicated niche space with solid engagement (4.55% average) on highly technical and specialized updates, like the CooRnet v2.0 release.
platformInsights.linkedin: Generates the highest absolute engagement numbers (averaging 90 interactions per post) due to its larger follower base, making it ideal for career updates and major project announcements.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
